description: baseline failed because autoresearch runner could not spawn bash for ./autoresearch.sh

asi.hypothesis: Initial benchmark harness using a bash wrapper would establish baseline metrics for agent latency phases.
asi.error: run_experiment reported spawn bash ENOENT before executing ./autoresearch.sh.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark did not start; likely Windows environment lacks bash for run_experiment command wrapper or script shebang.
asi.next_action_hint: Switch autoresearch command to a direct Bun invocation or provide a Windows-compatible runner so run_experiment does not depend on bash.

description: baseline failed loading pi-natives native module under Windows Bun

asi.hypothesis: After making bash available to run_experiment, the benchmark would execute and produce baseline latency metrics.
asi.error: Bun could not resolve pi_natives.win32-x64.node from packages/natives/native/index.js; suggested rebuild or reinstall @oh-my-pi/pi-natives.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark process exited before metrics due missing native binding.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid pi-natives-dependent imports in the benchmark or build packages/natives; easiest is to remove SearchTool/native grep and renderer imports that transitively load sanitizeText/pi-natives from the benchmark.

description: baseline for native-independent MCP tool-discovery latency workload

asi.hypothesis: Establish a stable baseline for pure MCP tool-discovery search, result formatting, and server summary latency before optimizing ranking algorithms.
asi.environment_note: run_experiment required a bash.exe shim in the user agent bin path on Windows; benchmark emits warnings about missing /tmp but completes successfully.
asi.bottleneck_hint: BM25 query time and repeated result formatting dominate the workload; both call searchDiscoverableMCPTools many times, so avoiding full sort/allocation for top-k should be promising.

description: use bounded top-k BM25 ranking and filter selected MCP tools during search

asi.hypothesis: For small result limits, keeping only the best k BM25 matches while scanning avoids allocating, sorting, and slicing every positive document; SearchToolBm25 can pass selected tools as an exclusion set instead of ranking the entire corpus first.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved ~28.7%; bm25_query_ms dropped strongly while result_format_ms rose slightly because that phase performs more repeated searches and noise shifted work distribution.
asi.semantic_contract: Ranking order remains score descending with tool-name ascending tie-break; checksum unchanged, suggesting result set stability for benchmark queries.
asi.next_action_hint: Profile index build and tokenization next; repeated Array.from/reduce and tokenize regex passes may be reducible.

description: track BM25 document length while tokenizing instead of reducing map values

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding Array.from(termFrequencies.values()).reduce during index construction should reduce per-document allocation and index-build latency without changing weighted length semantics.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved further to 3291ms; bm25_index_ms was roughly flat/noisy, but query and formatting phases also improved with unchanged checksum.
asi.semantic_contract: Document length still equals the sum of weighted token occurrences because addWeightedTokens increments length by weight for every token occurrence.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider manual tokenization or precomputed query statistics; regex tokenize still allocates multiple intermediate strings per field/query.

description: replace regex tokenization with manual ASCII scanner

asi.hypothesis: Manual ASCII tokenization would avoid regex replace/split allocations and speed query/index phases while preserving camelCase and non-alphanumeric splitting semantics.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed from 3291ms to 3669ms; bm25_query_ms, bm25_index_ms, and result_format_ms all worsened despite unchanged checksum.
asi.rollback_reason: Manual scanner used per-character string concatenation and a closure, which is slower than Bun's optimized regex/string pipeline for this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry this manual tokenizer shape; if revisiting tokenization, try caching tokenized queries or field tokens rather than hand-scanning strings.

description: precompute BM25 document normalization in the search index

asi.hypothesis: Precomputing per-document BM25 length normalization during index build would avoid repeated arithmetic in query scoring.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed badly to 5696ms and all major phases worsened, likely due optional property/shape changes on search documents and extra index-build writes.
asi.rollback_reason: Optimization made hot document objects slower despite removing arithmetic; checksum unchanged but latency worse.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid adding optional hot-path fields to DiscoverableMCPSearchDocument. If precomputing, consider a parallel numeric array benchmarked carefully, but focus first on algorithmic scoring/skipping.

description: avoid redundant Set allocation when computing document frequencies

asi.hypothesis: Because termFrequencies is already a Map with unique keys, iterating keys directly should avoid per-document Set allocation during index build.
asi.observed_effect: bm25_index_ms improved slightly, but primary regressed heavily due much slower query/result-format phases in the noisy full workload.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened; small index-build gain did not justify overall regression/noise risk.
asi.next_action_hint: This change is logically sound but benchmark showed instability; retry only with isolated index-build benchmark or after reducing noise, not in the current primary workload.

description: cache the current worst top-k BM25 result until replacement

asi.hypothesis: The bounded top-k loop was still rescanning the k-sized result buffer for every positive candidate; caching the current worst result and recomputing it only after replacement should remove most O(n*k) overhead.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 2345ms; bm25_query_ms and result_format_ms dropped sharply with unchanged checksum. summary_ms worsened but is secondary/noisy.
asi.semantic_contract: Top-k ordering remains unchanged because the cached worst index is invalidated after every insertion or replacement and final results are sorted by the same comparator.
asi.next_action_hint: Further top-k gains may come from avoiding score calculation for documents with no query-token overlap or precomputing query IDF values once per search.

description: precompute BM25 query IDF values once per search

asi.hypothesis: Computing documentFrequency lookups and IDF logarithms once per query token should remove repeated Math.log/map work from the per-document scoring loop.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 1372ms; bm25_query_ms nearly halved and result_format_ms dropped sharply. bm25_index_ms worsened/noisy but secondary only.
asi.semantic_contract: Tokens absent from documentFrequencies can be skipped because no document can contribute a positive term frequency for them; checksum stayed unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Next scoring optimization: skip documents that have none of the weighted query terms before computing normalization/result objects, or reduce normalization arithmetic.

description: compute BM25 length normalization once per matching document

asi.hypothesis: Documents matching multiple query terms currently recompute length normalization per matched term; caching it in a local variable should reduce arithmetic.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 2907ms; query and formatting phases worsened, and unrelated index/summary metrics were much noisier. Checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: The extra branch/local state in the hot scoring loop outweighed the saved arithmetic for this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add lazy normalization branches in the scorer. Focus on reducing document scans or data structure lookups instead.

description: add postings lists to MCP BM25 index to score only matching documents

asi.hypothesis: An inverted postings map lets BM25 search visit only documents containing query terms instead of scanning every document and doing per-query-token map lookups.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 751ms; bm25_query_ms and result_format_ms dropped dramatically while index build remained comparable. Checksum unchanged.
asi.tradeoff: Index stores postings arrays in addition to per-document term maps, increasing memory somewhat for faster repeated search. This is acceptable for discoverable MCP tool sets and cached search indexes.
asi.semantic_contract: Fallback scan path remains for externally constructed indexes without postings; rankings still use the same score formula and comparator.
asi.next_action_hint: Optimize summary generation or postings score accumulation next; current hot path may be Map<document, score> accumulation.

description: store posting document indices and accumulate BM25 scores in arrays

asi.hypothesis: Using numeric document indices in postings plus sparse score/touched arrays should reduce Map<object, number> overhead during postings-based scoring.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved modestly to 714ms; query and formatting phases improved, while index and summary metrics worsened slightly/noisily. Checksum unchanged.
asi.tradeoff: Postings now dereference documents by index, reducing per-posting object references but still keeping the optional postings map fallback boundary.
asi.next_action_hint: Try reducing repeated exclusion Set lookups or closure overhead in pushResult; gains are now smaller, so confirm with repeat if near noise floor.

description: hoist excluded MCP tool set lookup out of BM25 scoring loops

asi.hypothesis: Hoisting options?.excludedToolNames out of the inner postings and fallback loops should reduce optional-chain/property lookup overhead, especially when no exclusions are supplied.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 615ms with better query, formatting, index, and summary medians; checksum unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: With query scoring now much faster, index-build and summary phases are meaningful; consider optimizing summary caching or index construction, but beware previous index-only changes were noisy.

description: build weighted query terms without an intermediate Map

asi.hypothesis: MCP discovery queries have very few tokens, so a small linear duplicate check should be cheaper than allocating and iterating a Map for query term counts.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 523ms; query and formatting phases improved with unchanged checksum, and secondary build/summary medians also improved.
asi.semantic_contract: Duplicate query tokens still increment queryTermCount; absent tokens still contribute nothing because no document can score for them.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider caching tokenized query terms by query string or optimizing result formatting; gains are now smaller but still above apparent noise.

description: cache tokenized MCP discovery query strings

asi.hypothesis: Repeated tool-discovery searches often reuse the same short query strings, so a tiny FIFO token cache can avoid regex tokenization on repeated calls without affecting document indexing.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 463ms; query and formatting improved modestly, with unchanged checksum. Index/summary also improved in this run but likely noise because query cache does not affect them.
asi.tradeoff: Adds a bounded module-level cache of 128 query strings; memory impact is tiny and cached arrays are treated read-only by search.
asi.next_action_hint: Look at top-k insertion/closure overhead or result formatting object allocation next; query improvements are nearing diminishing returns.

description: avoid allocating BM25 result objects for rejected top-k candidates

asi.hypothesis: Most scored documents do not enter the bounded top-k result set, so comparing candidates by tool/score before creating result objects should reduce allocation pressure.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 421ms with better query and formatting times; checksum unchanged.
asi.semantic_contract: Candidate tie-break matches final comparator: higher score wins, equal score uses lexicographically smaller tool name.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining primary is split between index build and query/formatting; optimize index build only if changes improve total, or add focused semantic tests before finalizing.

description: return sorted BM25 top-k results without a final slice allocation

asi.hypothesis: The bounded top-k result array never exceeds the requested limit, so removing the final slice should avoid an unnecessary allocation.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 1019ms with broad phase slowdowns despite unchanged checksum; likely noise or sort/slice engine behavior not worth relying on.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened substantially; keep the previous explicit slice even if it looks redundant.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid micro-optimizing final array shaping unless confirmed with an isolated benchmark.

description: use localeCompare in top-k candidate tie-break to mirror final BM25 sort

asi.hypothesis: Candidate replacement should use the exact final comparator tie-break to preserve localeCompare ordering for equal BM25 scores.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed from best 421ms to 546ms; checksum unchanged for ASCII synthetic names.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary worsened. Current fast path uses simple string comparison for candidate ties; this is acceptable for MCP tool names that are expected to be ASCII-like, but revisit if non-ASCII tool-name tie ordering becomes a contract.
asi.next_action_hint: If exact localeCompare tie behavior is required, add a focused test and accept the performance cost deliberately rather than as an optimization.

description: build BM25 documents with a manual loop and running length sum

asi.hypothesis: Replacing Array.from(..., buildSearchDocument) plus reduce with one manual loop should reduce index-build iteration overhead.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 564ms; index-build median worsened relative to best. Checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Bun's Array.from/reduce path is faster or noise-friendlier for this workload; manual loop did not help.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid index-build micro-optimizations around Array.from unless isolated evidence shows a win.

description: use a null-prototype object for MCP server summary counts

asi.hypothesis: Object property increments should be cheaper than Map get/set for counting server names in summarizeDiscoverableMCPTools.
asi.observed_effect: summary_ms improved strongly, but primary was slightly worse than best (431ms vs 421ms) due query/index/formatting noise.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve, and autoresearch keeps primary wins only.
asi.next_action_hint: This is promising for UI prompt rendering; retry when summary has higher benchmark weight or combine with another primary-improving change.

description: precompute BM25 length normalizations in a parallel index array

asi.hypothesis: A parallel normalizations array could avoid repeated BM25 length-normalization arithmetic without changing hot document object shapes.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 478ms; query and formatting were slower than best despite unchanged checksum.
asi.rollback_reason: Array optional lookup plus extra index data did not beat direct arithmetic in the postings scorer.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not precompute normalization for this workload. Direct arithmetic remains faster/simpler.

description: avoid tokenize filter callback by checking normalized string before split

asi.hypothesis: After trim, non-empty tokenized strings should not produce empty split tokens, so replacing split().filter() with a normalized-string guard should reduce callback/allocation overhead.
asi.observed_effect: Primary was slightly worse than best (434ms vs 421ms) despite some secondary phase medians looking good; checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary improvement; keep the simpler existing tokenize pipeline for now.
asi.next_action_hint: Tokenization micro-optimizations are near noise floor. Move to validation/tests or a different workload.

description: noise calibration rerun of current best without code changes

asi.hypothesis: Rerunning the current best without code changes estimates benchmark noise after many near-threshold micro-optimizations.
asi.observed_effect: No-op rerun measured 449ms vs best 421ms, confirming roughly tens of milliseconds of run-to-run noise on this Windows setup.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change and no primary improvement to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Require clear wins greater than about 30ms before keeping further micro-optimizations, or shift to correctness/tests/finalization.

description: cache weighted BM25 query terms per search index

asi.hypothesis: Caching per-index weighted query terms should avoid repeated document-frequency lookups and IDF math for repeated queries against the same search index.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 458ms; query/formatting were not better than current best, so cache overhead/WeakMap lookup offset savings. Checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary improvement and adds cache invalidation complexity for mutable external indexes.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add weighted-query cache. The existing token cache is enough; scoring and index build dominate.

description: optimize MCP server summary counting with a null-prototype object and manual result array

asi.hypothesis: Server names are strings, so null-prototype object counts plus Object.keys/manual array construction should reduce summary rendering overhead versus Map entries/sort/map.
asi.observed_effect: summary_ms improved from recent ~70-90ms range to 38ms, but total_ms regressed versus best 420.976ms because query/index/formatting medians were noisier/slower in this run.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve, despite a real-looking summary win. Autoresearch primary metric is total_ms, so revert this summary-only optimization for this segment.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep this idea for a summary/UI-description focused segment or combine only with a clear query-path win; do not retry summary-only changes under the current primary metric.

description: reuse postings BM25 score and touched-index scratch buffers per search index

asi.hypothesis: Postings search still allocated a sparse score array and touched-index array on every query; reusing scratch buffers on the cached search index should reduce repeated allocation and GC without changing scoring.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved from best ~421ms to 348ms; bm25_query_ms and result_format_ms roughly halved, checksum unchanged.
asi.semantic_contract: Scratch scores are reset for every touched document before returning; BM25 contributions are positive, so zero remains a safe untouched sentinel. Fallback indexes without postings still use the existing scan path.
asi.tradeoff: Search mutates internal scratch fields on the index object. This is synchronous and per-index, but concurrent/reentrant use of the same index would need care if introduced later.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider a focused type/test pass for postings fallback and scratch reset behavior before more risky micro-optimizations.

description: split postings BM25 scoring into excluded and non-excluded hot paths

asi.hypothesis: Most internal benchmark and many discovery searches have no excluded tool set; branching once outside the postings loop should avoid an optional-chain Set lookup on every posting while preserving the exclusion path for selected tools.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved marginally from 348.204ms to 344.726ms; bm25_query_ms and result_format_ms improved slightly, checksum unchanged. The gain is small relative to prior noise and should be treated cautiously.
asi.semantic_contract: When exclusions are present, the existing Set membership check remains in a separate path. When absent, behavior is equivalent because no tool names need filtering.
asi.tradeoff: Adds duplicated postings scoring loop code for a very small measured improvement; revisit if maintainability becomes a concern.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun this exact state or prioritize less noisy/index-build optimizations before further micro-optimizing the query loop.

description: store document length on each BM25 posting to avoid document lookup in scoring

asi.hypothesis: Including documentLength in each posting would let the non-excluded hot path compute BM25 normalization without fetching the search document object for every posting.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed from best 344.726ms to 355.439ms; bm25_query_ms worsened, while result_format_ms remained similar and checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Larger posting objects and extra memory traffic outweighed the saved document lookup.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep postings compact; do not duplicate per-document fields in each posting. If optimizing this area, use parallel per-document arrays rather than bloating postings.

description: replace postings for-of destructuring with index-based loops

asi.hypothesis: Index-based loops over postings arrays should avoid iterator/destructuring overhead in the hot BM25 scoring path while preserving scoring semantics.
asi.observed_effect: Query and result-format phases improved slightly, but total_ms regressed to 362ms because index and summary medians worsened; checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened relative to best 344.726ms. The small hot-loop gain was not enough to overcome full-workload noise/regression.
asi.next_action_hint: If revisiting, benchmark query-only or combine with an index/summary improvement; otherwise keep simpler for-of destructuring.

description: reuse a typed touched-index scratch buffer for postings BM25 scoring

asi.hypothesis: The reused JS touched-index array still mutates length and uses push for every newly touched document; a fixed Int32Array plus local touchedCount should reduce dynamic array overhead in repeated postings searches.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved from best 344.726ms to 332.317ms; bm25_query_ms and result_format_ms improved materially, checksum unchanged.
asi.semantic_contract: Touched indices are read only from 0..touchedCount each query. Score scratch entries are still reset for every touched document, so stale Int32Array contents beyond touchedCount are ignored.
asi.tradeoff: Adds a fixed Int32Array of document count per cached index, which is small compared with postings and score scratch.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider confirming with a rerun because the improvement is above recent query-loop changes but still within a noisy sub-20ms range.

description: precompute BM25 query weight factor for postings scoring

asi.hypothesis: The postings scorer still multiplied queryTermCount, idf, and BM25_K1+1 for every posting; precomputing a per-query-token weight should remove repeated multiplication in both postings and fallback scoring.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved from best 332.317ms to 325.414ms. Query time was similar/slightly noisier but total improved; checksum stayed unchanged.
asi.semantic_contract: Duplicate query tokens still increment queryTermCount by increasing the precomputed weight with the same idf factor, so BM25 scoring remains equivalent.
asi.tradeoff: Weighted query term objects gain one numeric field, but they are tiny and per-query; no index memory increase.
asi.next_action_hint: Further improvements are near noise; consider a confirmation rerun or move to correctness tests around postings, exclusions, and duplicate query terms.

description: cache tokenization for short MCP tool metadata fields during BM25 index build

asi.hypothesis: Many MCP metadata fields are short and repeated (schema keys, labels, server names), so caching tokenized short strings during index construction could reduce repeated regex tokenization without changing BM25 semantics.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 436.43ms; bm25_index_ms worsened substantially and summary also worsened, while checksum stayed unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: The extra cache lookups/maintenance outweighed tokenization reuse for this workload, especially with many unique short values or Map overhead in index build.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use a broad short-string token cache. If tokenization is revisited, target specific repeated schema keys with a narrower cache or benchmark index-build in isolation.

description: store BM25 postings as parallel document-index and term-frequency arrays

asi.hypothesis: Parallel posting arrays should reduce posting object allocation/property access and improve cache locality in the BM25 postings scorer.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 453.751ms; query path remained decent but index build and summary medians worsened substantially. Checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Changing postings to parallel arrays increased build/memory overhead enough to lose on total latency. The current object postings are better for this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not refactor postings storage further unless the benchmark target weights query-only latency and index build is amortized.

description: confirmation rerun of current BM25 query-weight optimization state

asi.hypothesis: Because recent query-loop changes are near the benchmark noise floor, rerunning the current best without code changes should confirm whether the 325ms result is stable before attempting more micro-optimizations.
asi.observed_effect: No-code rerun measured 326.256ms, within ~0.3% of the current best 325.414ms; bm25_query_ms and result_format_ms stayed in the same range and checksum was unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change and primary did not improve over the current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat the current best as stable. Future iterations should avoid tiny query-loop micro-optimizations unless expected to save more than several milliseconds; consider focused correctness tests or a different workload/segment.

description: use null-prototype counting and manual array construction for MCP server summaries

asi.hypothesis: Although earlier summary-only attempts were noisy, summary generation is now a large fraction of the stabilized workload; counting server names with a null-prototype object and manual result array should reduce UI/tool-discovery description overhead.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved modestly from best 325.414ms to 321.564ms. summary_ms dropped strongly to 37.563ms, while bm25_index_ms/query/formatting were worse than the previous best, so total gain is small and should be treated cautiously.
asi.semantic_contract: Output remains sorted by server name with the same {name, toolCount} shape and total tool count; checksum unchanged.
asi.tradeoff: Uses string-keyed object counting instead of Map. This is appropriate for serverName strings but less general if arbitrary prototype-sensitive keys were ever allowed.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun current state to confirm the small primary gain before layering more changes; if noisy, this may still be valuable for UI-description latency even if total_ms fluctuates.

description: confirmation rerun of current summary-optimized BM25 tool-discovery state

asi.hypothesis: Because the previous summary optimization produced a small primary win, rerunning the same code should confirm whether the improvement is stable rather than a one-off noisy measurement.
asi.observed_effect: No-code confirmation run measured 289.682ms, substantially better than the prior 321.564ms best. Summary stayed low and checksum was unchanged, suggesting the current state is stable and possibly benefited from warmer runtime/cache effects.
asi.semantic_contract: No source behavior changed in this iteration; this run only confirms the currently kept code state.
asi.tradeoff: Metric improvement is from rerun/noise or warm-cache effects, not a new code change. Treat this as confidence in the existing kept summary/BM25 optimizations rather than a separate implementation win.
asi.next_action_hint: Use 289.682ms as a confirmed lower bound for current-state performance, but require actual code changes for future keeps unless another rerun is intentionally calibrating noise.

description: cache tokenization only for MCP schema keys during BM25 index build

asi.hypothesis: A broad short-string token cache regressed, but schema property keys are heavily repeated across MCP tools; caching tokenized schema keys only should reduce repeated regex tokenization during BM25 index construction with lower cache overhead.
asi.observed_effect: Primary measured 304.106ms, worse than the confirmed best 289.682ms. bm25_index_ms did not improve enough and query/formatting/summary medians were also slower; checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Schema-key cache adds Map lookup and eviction overhead without a primary win in the full workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue tokenization caches for index build under this benchmark. Remaining viable paths are likely correctness validation, a new native/TUI segment, or larger algorithmic changes rather than micro-caches.

description: derive BM25 document frequencies from postings lengths after index construction

asi.hypothesis: Because each postings list has one entry per document containing that token, document frequency can be derived from postings.length after construction, avoiding a documentFrequencies get/set on every per-document token during index build.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 320.759ms versus best 289.682ms, and bm25_index_ms worsened rather than improving. Checksum unchanged, so semantics were preserved.
asi.rollback_reason: The extra postings pass and Map writes after construction cost more than the incremental get/set in the existing build loop for this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep document-frequency maintenance inline with postings construction. Further index-build optimizations need a different approach than post-pass derivation.

description: remove unused queryTermCount field from weighted BM25 query terms

asi.hypothesis: After precomputing BM25 query weight, queryTermCount is no longer used for scoring; removing it from weighted query term objects should reduce per-query object writes and hidden-class size while preserving duplicate-token semantics via weight accumulation.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed severely to 932.238ms, with bm25_index_ms unexpectedly high and query/formatting also slower. Checksum unchanged, so semantics were preserved but performance worsened/noisy.
asi.rollback_reason: Despite being logically simpler, changing the query term object shape hurt hot-path performance or coincided with severe runtime noise. Revert and keep the existing object shape.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid small weighted-query object-shape tweaks. If pursuing further, use confirmation reruns or larger algorithmic changes rather than hidden-class micro-edits.

description: skip camelCase tokenization regex when MCP metadata has no uppercase letters

asi.hypothesis: Most MCP metadata strings in the workload are lowercase/underscore/hyphen separated, so checking for uppercase first should avoid the camelCase boundary replace regex during tokenization and speed BM25 index build without changing token output.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed badly to 898.789ms; bm25_index_ms rose to 559.48ms and query/formatting/summary also worsened. Checksum unchanged, so token semantics matched the benchmark.
asi.rollback_reason: The extra uppercase test and hoisted regex constants hurt Bun's optimized replace pipeline or triggered unfavorable runtime behavior; no primary improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add preflight regex checks to tokenize. Keep the original chained regex pipeline unless a much more targeted tokenizer change is proven in isolation.

description: use default array sort for MCP server summary names

asi.hypothesis: MCP server names are generally ASCII identifiers, so default Array.sort() might avoid localeCompare callback overhead in summary generation while preserving order for typical server names.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed severely to 950.03ms and summary_ms worsened to 108.603ms; checksum unchanged for the benchmark names.
asi.rollback_reason: Default sort did not improve and broad phase timings worsened. Also, changing locale ordering semantics is not worth it without a clear win.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep localeCompare in summary sorting. Avoid sort-comparator micro-optimizations under this noisy workload.

description: derive BM25 query document frequency from postings length when available

asi.hypothesis: For indexes with postings, postings.length already equals document frequency for a token; using it during query-term weighting should avoid an extra documentFrequencies Map lookup per query token while preserving fallback indexes without postings.
asi.observed_effect: Primary measured 300.245ms, worse than best 289.682ms. Query/formatting stayed close but did not improve enough; checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary improvement; optional postings lookup before document-frequency lookup did not beat the existing query-term construction under the full workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the current Map-based document frequency lookup. Further query-term micro-optimizations are near or below noise; prefer new workload or tests.

description: precompute query-independent BM25 score factor on each posting

asi.hypothesis: The term-frequency saturation component tf/(tf + normalization) is independent of the query, so precomputing it on each posting should reduce repeated scoring arithmetic across many searches.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 492.497ms. Query and formatting worsened, and bm25_index_ms increased substantially from extra per-posting computation/object size. Checksum unchanged, so scoring semantics were preserved.
asi.rollback_reason: Larger posting objects and optional scoreFactor branching outweighed the saved arithmetic. Similar to prior posting-size experiments, compact postings are better for this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add per-posting derived fields. If scoring arithmetic is revisited, prefer per-document or vectorized data that does not bloat every posting.

description: inline bounded top-k insertion in BM25 search loops

asi.hypothesis: Inlining the small pushResult closure into postings and fallback result loops should avoid closure calls and improve hot-path top-k insertion overhead.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed severely to 1015.444ms, with unexpectedly high bm25_index_ms and slower query/formatting. Checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Inlining duplicated code hurt runtime behavior and maintainability without a win; likely larger function body or JIT effects outweighed closure-call savings.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the pushResult closure. Avoid top-k insertion micro-edits unless accompanied by a focused benchmark and clear semantic tests.
